channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 Drone Swarms Packed Into Unassuming Containers Sought By DARPA | The War Zone
item.description: Remotely triggered containerized drone swarms, potentially placed behind enemy lines, create a hard-to-defend-against threat from pretty much everywhere. The post Drone Swarms Packed Into Unassuming Containers Sought By DARPA appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Digest of Recent Articles on Just Security (May 4-8, 2026) | Just Security
item.description: U.S.-Israel-Iran War • Russia-Ukraine War • U.S. Military • U.S. Boat Strikes • Series: Syria in Transition • Series: When Guardrails Erode • Violent Extremism • AI Governance • Hungary • International Human Rights • Trump Executive Actions The post Digest of Recent Articles on…

item.title: 🌍 F/A-18 Super Hornet Drops Bombs Down Smoke Stacks Of Iranian Tankers Running Blockade (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: The attack comes as the UAE claims it was struck again by Iran and hours after the U.S. and Iran exchanged blows. The post F/A-18 Super Hornet Drops Bombs Down Smoke Stacks Of Iranian Tankers Running Blockade (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 So What’s the Strategy for China? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Host Matthew Kroenig is joined by Kurt Campbell, chairman and co-founder of The Asia Group, to discuss former President Joe Biden’s strategy toward China, the Trump administration's strategic approach to Beijing, and Donald Trump’s upcoming meeting with Chinese President Xi…

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine ramps up ground robot production to spare soldiers, haul ammo — and rescue grandma | Defense News
item.description: The same unmanned ground systems Ukraine uses to haul ammunition and evacuate wounded soldiers are now pulling civilians out of contested ground.

item.title: 📍 Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week | The War Zone
item.description: Concrete is the new black. The post Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Russia, Ukraine to enter temporary ceasefire with prisoner exchange, Trump says | Defense News
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that Russia and Ukraine would temporarily halt fighting for three days beginning Saturday.

item.title: 📍 Can Friedrich Merz’s chancellorship survive? | Atlantic Council
item.description: One year into his chancellorship, Friedrich Merz heads a government beset by economic stagnation, low public approval, and coalition tensions. The post Can Friedrich Merz’s chancellorship survive? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Southeast Asian Leaders Tackle Iran War Vulnerabilities | Foreign Policy
item.description: Global market shocks spark calls for a regional power grid and emergency fuel stockpile.

item.title: 🌏 What in the World? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Test yourself on the week of May 2: The U.S. announces a drawdown in Germany, Taiwan’s president concludes a foreign visit, and Sudan lobs accusations at Ethiopia.

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth aims to cut through the bureaucracy with ‘Deal Team Six’ | Defense News
item.description: The Pentagon is utilizing a crew of private sector businessmen to renovate how the department negotiates with defense companies.

item.title: 📍 A Foreign Correspondent of Animal Kingdoms | Foreign Policy
item.description: How a headstrong field biologist helped birth the worldwide conservation movement.

item.title: 🕵️ How to Choose a Gift for Trump | Foreign Policy
item.description: An expert in diplomatic gift-giving describes the use and abuse of official presents.

item.title: 🏴 Poland becomes first nation to sign EU SAFE loans, expects billions for defense | Breaking Defense
item.description: Poland is now on track to receive over $51 billion in EU loans for its military, after overcoming domestic political hurdles.

item.title: 🔐 A Confused ‘Animal Farm’ for a Confused Time | Foreign Policy
item.description: What the latest Orwell adaptation says about our politics.

item.title: ⚛️ What Is Behind Turkey’s Pursuit Of An ICBM? | The War Zone
item.description: An unexpected reveal of a previously unknown intercontinental ballistic missile has raised major questions about Ankara’s strategic ambitions. The post What Is Behind Turkey’s Pursuit Of An ICBM? appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Costa Rica’s paradox of political continuity | Atlantic Council
item.description: Across Latin America, voters have punished incumbents and rejected traditional parties. Costa Rica appears to be an exception. The post Costa Rica’s paradox of political continuity appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 Why an Australia-US Rare Earth Deal Sparked Backlash in Malaysia | The Diplomat
item.description: There are worries that processing operations in the country will drag Malaysia into the middle of China-U.S. competition.

item.title: 📍 The Newly Released Government UFO Archives Will Leave You Shrugging | The War Zone
item.description: The latest release of records follows years of muddled messaging and controversial claims on a topic with real national security implications. The post The Newly Released Government UFO Archives Will Leave You Shrugging appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🔐 1 Campaign, 2 Targets: China’s Cyber Operations Hit Asian Governments and Dissidents Abroad | The Diplomat
item.description: One track pursued traditional intelligence collection against Asian governments and defense entities; the other sought to surveil and silence overseas critics.

item.title: 📍 Trump’s Tariffs Just Got Struck Down in Court—Again | Foreign Policy
item.description: Courts keep taking a skeptical view of the executive branch’s overreach.

item.title: 🏛️ Turkish air force contracts its first batch of indigenous KAAN jets | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The initial sale was made for the first batch of 20 Block 10 aircraft. This is the first order. Over time, we expect the numbers to increase,” Turkish Aerospace CEO Mehmet Demiroglu told Breaking Defense.

item.title: 🌍 US forces disable Iranian-flagged tankers trying to cross blockade | Defense News
item.description: This week, U.S. forces around the Strait of Hormuz have launched strikes against Iranian military facilities and disabled missiles launched by the IRGC.

item.title: 🌍 The Surprisingly Normal Streets of Tehran | Foreign Policy
item.description: U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran have led regular people to rally around the flag—for now.

item.title: 📍 First-of-its-kind electromagnetic spectrum exercise tests senior leaders in Arctic conditions | Breaking Defense
item.description: Aurora Pulse focused top operational and planning staffs of combatant commands on how to fight in a degraded spectrum environment in the Arctic.

item.title: 🌏 In a Single Day, 3 Democracies Pushed Back Against Chinese Transnational Repression | The Diplomat
item.description: That three liberal democracies acted against Beijing’s long arm on a single day was not coordinated – but perhaps it should have been.

item.title: 🏛️ Marines to phase out F/A-18 maintenance jobs as Hornet era ends | Defense News
item.description: Enlisted Marines with Hornet specialties will be offered retraining, reassignment or career dead ends.

item.title: 🌏 The US and China Don’t Need Another Dialogue. They Need a Circuit Breaker. | The Diplomat
item.description: A proposed Board of Trade will matter only if it can keep ordinary commercial disputes from becoming tests of national resolve.

item.title: 📍 Chinese Fighter Sales Surged After the 2025 India-Pakistan Aerial Clashes | The Diplomat
item.description: The India-Pakistan clashes gave the J-10C a new, highly coveted status: battled-tested.

item.title: 📍 Kroenig quoted in The Wall Street Journal on Rubio’s trip to Italy | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 7, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig was quoted in The Wall Street Journal about Secretary of State Marco Rubio's trip to Italy, arguing that he is the Trump administration's most effective foreign policy communicator.…
